Hand type ev (#40)

- Only count villain fold/call/raise when hero bets or raises (not checks)
- Equity vs full range still uses all hands (for EV of passive action)
- Add HandEvPanel component with curated 18-row hand type list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix lexer regex patterns to support player names with spaces

- Update SEAT_STACK_RE, COLLECTED_RE, SHOWS_RE, SUMMARY_COLLECTED_RE to use
  (.+?) instead of (\S+) so multi-word player names are captured correctly
- Replace SEAT_RE in core.py with SEAT_STACK_RE import to avoid matching
  summary lines as false player entries
- Add SHOWS_RE matching in SHOWDOWN state so shown cards are parsed when
  they appear after the *** SHOWDOWN *** marker
- Add test_lexer.py with 15 tests covering spaced names in seats, actions,
  collections, shown cards, and flop_player_count

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix pool hand detection for bought hands with no Hero player

build_context() set hero_sees_flop=True for bought hands because
'Hero never folded' (Hero never existed). This bypassed the pool
analysis path entirely, generating phantom non-pool FlopEvents and
no LineEvents.

Fix: check that the perspective player actually exists in the hand's
player list before determining if they saw the flop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add per-file pickle cache for hand history parsing

- Extract parse_file() from parse_histories() to process a single file
  and return a FileParseResult with all events + hand dates
- Eliminate parse_hand_dates() double-read: dates are now collected in
  the same single pass as events (using include_multiway=True)
- Add per-file pickle cache in hand_histories/.cache/ -- on startup each
  file is checked against its cache; only new/modified files are parsed
- Subsequent startups load from cache in seconds instead of re-parsing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log each file loaded from cache

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix double-load: use uvicorn factory to create app once

Previously create_app() ran at module import, then uvicorn re-imported
the module to resolve the app reference, causing a second load. Using
factory=True defers app creation to uvicorn's single worker init.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor: single-pass aggregation for all store methods

- Rewrite Flops.json(), bet_sizes() to single-pass with counters
- Rewrite _compute_flop/turn/river_stats() from 10/6/6 passes to 1
- Rewrite spot_stats() tail loops from 6 to 1, use heapq for top wins/losses
- Merge pot_bb and hero_faces_bet into ev_node_data() main loop
- Add date range params to all store methods (json, spot_stats, ev_node_data, hand_type_distribution)
- Eliminate router pre-copy pattern: all routers pass date range directly
- Add flops/turns/rivers instances to EventStore, populated in loader
- Rewrite Turns.json() and Rivers.json() to single-pass with date range

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace in-memory stores with SQLite database

- Add backend/app/db/ module: schema.py (tables + indexes), importer.py
  (parse -> INSERT with bulk transactions), queries.py (SQL aggregation)
- Schema: 5 event tables + file_imports tracker, composite indexes on
  filter columns (is_pool, pot_type, board_type, position, date)
- Line events store action lists as JSON strings, precompute 18 hand
  classification columns per street for equity/hand type queries
- Flop/Turn/River queries use pure SQL COUNT/SUM/GROUP BY
- Line event queries use SQL for filtering, Python for action prefix
  matching and equity calculations on the filtered subset
- Replace pickle cache entirely -- DB tracks imported files by mtime
- Update all routers to use get_db dependency + query functions
- Remove EventStore dataclass and in-memory store logic
- All 76 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify import progress logs to 'x/y files parsed'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix BoardType serialization: use .value not int()

BoardType is Enum not IntEnum, so int() fails on it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SQLite threading: allow cross-thread connection use

FastAPI runs endpoints in a thread pool, but SQLite connections
are thread-bound by default. Set check_same_thread=False.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ranges enum deserialization + add DB round-trip tests

Ranges router now converts DB strings back to PokerPosition/PotType
enums before passing to Ranges.add_hand().

Add tests/test_db.py with 16 tests covering:
- RangeEvent position/pot_type round-trip and enum conversion
- FlopEvent board_type storage and BoardType filter
- TurnEvent GROUP BY action sequence
- RiverEvent showdown stats
- LineEvent action strings, precomputed hand types, card storage
- Volume date grouping and filtering
- Pool event include/exclude

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix heapq crash on equal pnl values + defer card parsing to top N

heapq compared dicts when two hands had the same pnl. Now uses
(pnl, index) tuples so the integer tiebreaker prevents dict comparison.

Also defers hand_result dict construction (with expensive card parsing)
to only the final top 5 wins/losses instead of every row.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix query_spot_stats response shape to match frontend API contract

The frontend expects nested ev_stats, street_stats, bet_sizes,
next_actor, action_depth, turn_complete, top_wins/top_losses.
query_spot_stats was returning a flat structure with different field
names. Now matches the old stores.py spot_stats response exactly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Infrastructure: WAL mode, connection-per-request, column migration

- Switch SQLite journal_mode from DELETE to WAL for concurrent readers
- Replace shared app.state.db with app.state.db_path + fresh connection
  per request (thread-safe, no check_same_thread needed)
- Add backend/migrate.py: adds 21 new columns to line_events and
  backfills from JSON action strings (39s for 2M rows)
  - 12 street stats booleans (turn/river hero/villain bet/fold/raise)
  - 9 first-action columns (flop/turn/river first actor/action/size)
- Update schema.py, importer.py to include new columns for fresh DBs
- All 93 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add split SQL endpoints for line analyser panels

New pure SQL query functions:
- query_street_stats: SUM(cbet/donk) for flop, denormalised booleans
  for turn/river (~1.3s for 2M rows, 17ms for 3K)
- query_ev_summary: COUNT/SUM + GROUP BY for next_actions, bet_sizes
  (~4.8s for 2M rows, 41ms for 3K)
- query_top_hands: ORDER BY hero_pnl_bb LIMIT 5 (~1.5s for 2M rows)
- query_hand_type_distribution: pure SQL GROUP BY on precomputed columns

Infrastructure:
- _build_action_where: converts action prefix to json_extract SQL WHERE
- _action_prefix_where: combines flop/turn/river action WHERE clauses
- _combined_line_where: merges filter + action prefix WHERE clauses
- _where_and: safely appends conditions to WHERE strings

query_ev_node_data also updated to use SQL action prefix filtering
instead of Python _filter_by_street_prefix.

New router endpoints: /street-stats, /ev-summary, /top-hands
Legacy monolithic endpoint preserved for backwards compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Frontend: parallel panel fetching with split endpoints

- Replace monolithic getLineAnalysis with 5 parallel API calls:
  getStreetStats, getEvSummary, getTopHands, getHandTypeDistribution,
  getHandEv -- each fires independently and updates state as it resolves
- Extract shared LineAnalysisParams interface and buildLineAnalysisSearchParams
  helper, eliminating duplicated param building across 5 API functions
- Add AbortController support: stale requests are cancelled on filter change,
  preventing slow pool queries from overwriting newer results
- Add TopHandsPanel (biggest wins/losses) to the grid layout
- Single useEffect with lineParams useMemo replaces 3 duplicate effects

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix get_db: yield+close connections, drop per-request WAL pragma

The per-request PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL was a write operation that
could conflict with concurrent reads under 5 parallel requests.
WAL is already set once at init_db() and persists per-database.

Changed get_db to a generator that yields the connection and closes
it in the finally block, preventing connection leaks. Added
PRAGMA query_only=ON for safety.

Added 5 regression tests for split endpoints (street-stats,
ev-summary, top-hands, ev-node-data with pool+all filters).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SQLite cross-thread close: add check_same_thread=False

FastAPI runs dependency cleanup (conn.close()) in a different thread
than the one that created and used the connection. SQLite blocks this
by default. Safe here because each request gets its own connection
and WAL mode handles concurrent reads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add solver ML backend: model, training pipeline, ranges, and EV calculator

Phase 2 - Training Data Pipeline:
- solver/dataset.py: Token/TrainingExample types, extract_training_examples()
  from HandAST, PokerDataset, collate_fn with dynamic padding
- solver/augmentation.py: suit permutation (24x), flop reorder (6x),
  hole card swap (4x) = 576x augmentation per hand

Phase 3 - Solver Backend:
- solver/model.py: SolverTransformer (64-dim, 4 heads, 4 layers, 206K params)
  with heterogeneous token embeddings (cards, actions, street markers)
- solver/train.py: full training loop with AdamW, train/val split,
  augmented training, checkpoint saving
- solver/ranges.py: GTO preflop ranges per position/pot type, range
  filtering by equity into raise/call/fold sub-ranges
- solver/ev_calculator.py: solve_spot() using model predictions for
  villain response, equity-based EV computation per hero hand

Phase 4 - Integration:
- __init__.py loads model checkpoint at startup if available
- solver.py endpoint auto-selects real model vs mock fallback
- All 115 existing tests pass, frontend builds clean

Multi-operator architecture and CertificateStore simplification

- Multiple operator containers can now feed a single proxy, each handling
  one domain with its own YARP config file and certificate subdirectory
- Docker containers must set fennath.domain label for operator affinity
- Domain-scoped route/cluster IDs prevent collisions across operators
- Proxy loads multiple YARP config files via YarpConfigPaths list
- CertificateStore is now proxy-only: reads all PFX files from disk,
  provides memoized SNI-based cert selection with wildcard matching
- Operator writes certs directly via CertificateRenewalService (no
  CertificateStore dependency)
- CertificateFileWatcher handles Renamed events for atomic writes and
  does initial reload after attaching
- Fallback cert uses static CN=placeholder for unmatched SNI hostnames
- CertificateStoreOptions simplified to StoragePath only
- ADR-018 documents the multi-operator deployment model
- 91 tests passing (25 new: domain filter, scoped IDs, multi-cert SNI)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: include date in actions/cache key to prevent stale restores

The previous key (metrics-data-{owner}) had no date component, so the
cache always restored data from a prior day. loadCache() would then
reject it (date mismatch or missing weeklyTrends), making the restore
step a no-op and always triggering a full fresh collection.

The key now includes today's UTC date so:
- First run of the day: cache miss -> fresh collection -> data saved
- Same-day re-runs / dispatches: cache hit -> loadCache() accepts it -> API skipped

Removed the restore-keys fallback since restoring old-dated data
serves no purpose.

fix: use original image for AI vision path, not binarized version

GPT-4o vision works better with natural color/grayscale images.
The binarization pipeline (adaptive threshold) was designed for
Tesseract OCR and distorts digits/letters when applied to images
sent to the vision model — causing misreads that look like invented data.

- Add preprocessForAI(): resize only (max 2048px), JPEG 0.95, no
  grayscale/binarization/deskew — preserves original color and detail
- callParseInvoiceAI(): use preprocessForAI instead of preprocessForOCR

commit.message:
Fix stuck orchestrate poller when wave has uncreated issues with no pending definitions (#716)

When wave issues have github_issue == null and no pending_issue_defs exist,
the deferred creation mechanism cannot create them. Previously, the poller
would loop forever: check-wave-status reported wave_complete=false (because
not_created entries block all_merged), stall detection skipped null entries,
and the judge only ran on wave_complete=true.

Now the poller detects this stuck state after terminal status persistence:
if all created (non-null) issues are terminal and remaining null issues
have no pending definitions, it invokes the judge to resolve the situation
(e.g., define missing issues, skip them, or take corrective action).
The judge prompt includes explicit context about the stuck wave.

* docs: add tech debt audit report and implementation plan

backend (6 Rust crates), and cross-cutting concerns. Reviewed by Opus 4.6,

with 25 tasks.

* fix: align feature flag defaults between Rust and TypeScript

Feature flags export_view, mcp_servers, and skills had different default
values in Rust (config.rs) vs TypeScript (defaults.ts/preferences.ts).
This caused users to see different feature availability depending on
whether Rust or TS defaults loaded first.

- Extract DEFAULT_FEATURES constant as single source of truth in TS
- Align TS defaults to match Rust: exportView=false, mcpServers=true, skills=true
- Preferences store now derives initial values from DEFAULT_FEATURES
- Hydration fallbacks use spread instead of per-field ?? operators

Fixes tech-debt-consolidation-report.md §7.2

* fix: persist repo scope across skills store mutations

loadSkills(repoRoot?) accepted an optional repo filter, but 9 mutation
functions called loadSkills() without it after create/update/rename/
import operations. This caused the skills list to reload with all
skills instead of the scoped view.

Store now persists currentRepoRoot internally and uses it as the default
for subsequent reloads. Callers can still override by passing a new
repoRoot explicitly.

Fixes tech-debt-consolidation-report.md §3.6

commit.message:
fix(middleware): add per-tool-type frequency detection to LoopDetectionMiddleware (#1988)

* fix(middleware): add per-tool-type frequency detection to LoopDetectionMiddleware

The existing hash-based loop detection only catches identical tool call
sets. When the agent calls the same tool type (e.g. read_file) on many
different files, each call produces a unique hash and bypasses detection.
This causes the agent to exhaust recursion_limit, consuming 150K-225K
tokens per failed run.

Add a second detection layer that tracks cumulative call counts per tool
type per thread. Warns at 30 calls (configurable) and forces stop at 50.
The hard stop message now uses the actual returned message instead of a
hardcoded constant, so both hash-based and frequency-based stops produce
accurate diagnostics.

Also fix _apply() to use the warning message returned by
_track_and_check() for hard stops, instead of always using _HARD_STOP_MSG.

Closes #1987

* Apply suggestions from code review

* feat: §29.8.3 module visibility per project (#263)

Add per-project module visibility configuration. Projects can specify
which modules are hidden from the activity bar. Hidden modules remain
accessible via direct URL — only the nav icon is suppressed.

Backend:
- New IModuleVisibilityStore interface (Domain)
- SqliteModuleVisibilityStore with transactional replace (Infrastructure)

- Audit logging for visibility changes

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

- src/frontend/src/modules/hooks.ts — useProjectScopedState hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/index.ts — barrel export for hook

- src/frontend/src/test/projectCache.test.ts (new) — 12 tests
